text: Writing clean code is essential for any developer, as it makes your work easier for others and for your future self to understand. Readable code relies on clear naming conventions; for example, use descriptive variable names like `totalSales` instead of `ts`. Keep your functions small and focused on a single task. This approach improves maintainability because when you need to fix a bug or add a new feature, you can quickly find and update the relevant part of your code. By following these practices, you save time and reduce frustration in the long run, creating software that is both efficient and easy to manage.
